name: Dolomite Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Dolomite. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal power for stone surface restoration

power.description: Typical range for industrial cleaning of carbonate stones like dolomite to remove contaminants without thermal damage; start at 150W for controlled ablation. Max safe ~300-500W depending on fluence. Based on manufacturer specs from IPG Photonics and Coherent for 1064 nm systems used in heritage stone cleaning (e.g., avoids decomposition above 900°C).

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds for 1064 nm cleaning of dolomite; slower for heavy contamination (500-1000 mm/s), faster for light (2000-5000 mm/s). Recommended 1500 mm/s for optimal contaminant removal with minimal passes. From industry standards in laser cleaning manuals (e.g., Adapt Laser Systems) and studies on stone surface preparation.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for cost-effective cleaning of dolomite; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond for ultra-minimal HAZ but less common industrially. 20 ns recommended to limit heat diffusion in low-conductivity dolomite. Supported by laser physics research in Optics & Lasers in Engineering (e.g., ns pulses reduce thermal effects in carbonates).

repetition_rate.description: Typical for 1064 nm cleaning to balance speed and heat; higher rates (50-100 kHz) enable faster processing but risk accumulation in low-thermal-conductivity dolomite. 50 kHz recommended for safe operation. From academic literature like Journal of Applied Physics on pulse train effects in stone ablation.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for contaminant removal on dolomite; damage threshold ~2-4 J/cm² (avoids carbonate decomposition). 1.5 J/cm² recommended for safe cleaning. Based on ablation threshold studies in Applied Surface Science (e.g., 1064 nm on calcite/dolomite analogs, conservative due to reflectivity ~0.2-0.3 at IR).

spot_size.description: Beam diameter for precise vs. broad cleaning; smaller for fine details on dolomite surfaces (100-200 μm), larger for areas (300-500 μm). 200 μm recommended for uniform preparation. From equipment specs in Laserax and SPI Lasers systems for industrial stone applications.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass (2-5) for heavy; 2 passes recommended for complete removal on dolomite without over-ablation. Drawn from industry case studies in heritage conservation (e.g., ICSC World Heritage reports on laser cleaning of carbonate stones).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage; 30-50% for speed, 60-80% for thoroughness on irregular dolomite surfaces. 60% recommended to prevent gaps in cleaning. From process optimization studies in Lasers in Engineering journal for scanned beam cleaning of minerals.
